rawResponse.headline: Sam gave only one-word answers all session — this is a partial read, not a verdict on pace, clarity, or engagement.
summary_bullets: Nine straight turns stayed at "fine," "ok," "i guess," "not sure," and "dunno" — the record is too thin to read what landed for Sam.
The only clear signal is the silence itself: no friction, priorities, or lift got named, so the check-in never got past surface level.
rawResponse.understanding_paragraph: We did not learn what actually landed for Sam, what snagged, or what she wants next from this fortnight. The session showed a non-functional read: every turn stayed at one or two words, so there is no reliable story beyond the lack of detail.

axes.meaning: This didn't come up in the conversation — not enough signal to read.
This didn't come up in the conversation — not enough signal to read.
This didn't come up in the conversation — not enough signal to read.
This didn't come up in the conversation — not enough signal to read.

rawResponse.brutal_truth_employee: Too few turns hold a real note on Sam to call this a read. What we do have is a wall of "fine," "ok," "i guess," "not sure," and "dunno," which says nothing beyond Sam not opening up here.
rawResponse.brutal_truth_manager: You kept moving past "i guess" and "not sure" instead of stopping to pin down one concrete example. The turn after "dunno" was the moment to switch from broad check-in to a specific prompt about one thing that landed or snagged.

next_actions.action: Re-ask the landing question with one concrete example prompt: "what shipped, what didn't, and what was the one snag?"
Set up a shorter follow-up with a tighter prompt list and no broad opener.

watch_for: Before next 1:1: if Sam stays at one-word answers again, treat this as a conversation problem, not a work update.
Within two weeks: ask for one specific thing that landed and one thing that snagged; if neither comes, the read is still missing.

engagement_read.missing_evidence: Need at least one full answer with a concrete work example, preference, or concern.
engagement_read.recommended_action: Ask one narrow question and wait for a specific example before moving on.
engagement_read.watch_next: Sam gives a sentence with a named project, snag, or choice instead of a one-word reply.

managerNotes: Regular fortnightly check-in. Want to see how the last two weeks have landed.

focusPoints.label: What shipped and what landed in the last two weeks.
focusPoints.reason: Whether the last two weeks’ work is landing as intended, and what they’re choosing to deprioritize next.

focusPoints.label: What’s slowing their design work down right now.
focusPoints.reason: If anything is stuck—decisions, dependencies, or unclear requirements—this is the fastest way to unblock mid-level delivery.

focusPoints.label: What they want more from you this cycle.
focusPoints.reason: How they’re using this check-in to ask for air cover, coaching, or time from you before the next iteration starts.

question.label: Opening pace check
question.name: How's the last two weeks actually felt — energy-wise?
question.description: Opener that invites a real answer rather than 'fine'. Reads wellbeing directly.

transcript.note: [SHALLOW] 'fine' is a one-word non-answer with no specifics about energy or what shaped the last two weeks.

question.label: Prep opener
question.name: How have the last couple of weeks felt from your side, and what actually landed the way you expected?
question.description: Gives Sam room to set the scene before you get into what to keep moving or let wait.

transcript.note: [SHALLOW] 'ok' is a one-word non-answer with no specifics.

question.label: Clarify ok
question.name: When you say 'ok', what actually landed in the last two weeks?
question.description: Re-prompts the shallow answer so Sam names one concrete thing that landed.

transcript.note: [SHALLOW x2 — advancing] [SHALLOW] 'i guess' is a non-answer with no specifics, so no signal on clarity or engagement.

question.label: Friction scan
question.name: What's been slower or harder than it should have been?
question.description: Invites them to name blockers without framing it as complaint.

transcript.note: [SHALLOW] 'not sure' is a non-answer with no specific friction named.

question.label: Name the snag
question.name: When you say 'not sure', where has work felt most snaggy lately?
question.description: One-shot re-prompt to surface a concrete point of friction.

transcript.note: [SHALLOW] 'dunno' is a non-answer with no concrete work signal; [SESSION NON-FUNCTIONAL: 3+ consecutive non-answers. Queue cleared.]

question.label: Reset check-in
question.name: Is now a good time for this conversation, or should we pick another time?
question.description: Direct reset after repeated non-answers to check whether the session can continue productively.

transcript.note: [SHALLOW] 'nothing to add' is a non-answer, and [SESSION NON-FUNCTIONAL: 3+ consecutive non-answers. Queue cleared.]

question.label: Success definition
question.name: What would a good quarter look like for you from here?
question.description: Surfaces how clearly they picture a good outcome, without forcing a one-line answer.

transcript.note: [SESSION NON-FUNCTIONAL: 3+ consecutive non-answers. Queue cleared.] [SHALLOW] 'no' is a non-answer with no usable signal. [BUDGET-STARVED]

question.label: Priority clarity
question.name: What are you actually focused on this week, and why those things?
question.description: Surfaces whether priorities are crisp or vague; whether they match what you thought.

transcript.note: [SHALLOW] 'same' gives no specific priority or rationale; [SESSION NON-FUNCTIONAL: 3+ consecutive non-answers. Queue cleared.]

question.label: Next two weeks
question.name: What would make the next two weeks feel lighter for you?
question.description: Ends the check-in on a concrete change you can help with.

transcript.note: [SHALLOW] 'fine' is a one-word non-answer with no specifics. [SESSION NON-FUNCTIONAL: 3+ consecutive non-answers. Queue cleared.]

bankQuestions.label: Prep opener
bankQuestions.name: How have the last couple of weeks felt from your side, and what actually landed the way you expected?
bankQuestions.description: Gives Sam room to set the scene before you get into what to keep moving or let wait.

bankQuestions.label: What shipped landed
bankQuestions.name: Which design decision from the last two weeks felt like it really held up?
bankQuestions.description: Helps you hear what part of the recent work should stay in the mix.

bankQuestions.label: Scope shift point
bankQuestions.name: Where did scope shift on you in the last fortnight?
bankQuestions.description: Lets you hear where the work moved under him and what that changed.

bankQuestions.label: Waiting on others
bankQuestions.name: What are you waiting on that is holding the design work up right now?
bankQuestions.description: Shows you what is stuck without turning the check-in into a blocker audit.

bankQuestions.label: Keep moving
bankQuestions.name: What do you want to keep moving next, and what can wait?
bankQuestions.description: Helps Sam name the real trade-off in the next stretch of work.

bankQuestions.label: Design decision load
bankQuestions.name: Which decision from the last two weeks took the most out of you?
bankQuestions.description: Helps you hear where the work is costing more energy than it should.

bankQuestions.label: Feedback that stuck
bankQuestions.name: What feedback from the last two weeks actually changed the design?
bankQuestions.description: Shows whether feedback is shaping the work or just adding noise.

bankQuestions.label: User flow confidence
bankQuestions.name: Which user flow feels most settled now?
bankQuestions.description: Helps you hear where the design has real shape versus still needing attention.

bankQuestions.label: Manager support ask
bankQuestions.name: What would you want more from me on before the next round starts?
bankQuestions.description: Makes space for a direct ask about support, time, or coaching.

bankQuestions.label: Next two weeks
bankQuestions.name: What would make the next two weeks feel lighter for you?
bankQuestions.description: Ends the check-in on a concrete change you can help with.
